code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-FEB-2022Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Mild metabolic acidosis is indicated by low serum CO₂ and elevated chloride. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and underlying etiology.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
